reason:
Set 094 Session 2 close — attested verification override (--manual-verify).

Verification ran via API (task_type=session-verification, verifier gpt-5-6,
anthropic excluded), two rounds. Round 1 and Round 2 EACH surfaced exactly one
Major, both on the shipping extension README's onboarding sequence: it described
a circular first-run path (the per-module row actions were said to create the
first session set, yet the Work Explorer tree that carries those rows only
replaces the Getting Started form once >=1 session set already exists).

Resolution (in flight): traced selectExplorerMode in
src/utils/gettingStartedDetection.ts (form while zero sets; tree once >=1 set)
and rewrote the README to the ACTUAL behavior — the first plan + first session
set are created from the Command Palette ("Dabbler: Import Project Plan" and
"Dabbler: Generate Session-Set Prompt"), which work while the form is up; the
per-module row actions are for adding MORE sets once the tree appears. This is
exactly the corrected sequence the verifier itself prescribed.

At the 2-round automatic verification cap (Set 086 operator rule: past two
automatic rounds, an unfixed/agreed material Critical/Major stops to the human
to adjudicate rather than auto-opening a third round), the operator ADJUDICATED
the fix as resolving the Major and directed close (AskUserQuestion → "Accept
fix, close adjudicated"), declining a confirming round 3. The finding was not
disputed; the fix is doc-only, zero code risk, and was verified against the
actual mode-selection code.

The advisory path-aware critique (openai gpt-5.4 + google gemini-2.5-pro, both
distinct from the orchestrator provider) returned VERIFIED with zero findings.
Suite green at close: extension unit 1487, Playwright 26, pytest 2922/6, tsc
clean.

Disposition verdict: ISSUES_FOUND_RESOLVED_IN_FLIGHT. The immutable raw
artifacts (s2-verification*.md / s2-issues*.json) preserve the full ISSUES_FOUND
history. This attestation records the operator's adjudication as the
verification-evidence-layer resolution in lieu of a machine-confirmed VERIFIED
round.
